topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp white envelope felt heavy in Amelia’s hand, the paper slightly too thick for a simple notification. Her fingers traced the embossed seal, a cold prickle running up her arm. Inside, the results were stark: a match. A genetic match to a family she had never known. Amelia reread the report, the words swimming before her eyes, each line a shard of unexpected reality. She stood by the window, the city lights blurring into streaks of indifferent color. Her breath hitched, a shallow, quick intake of air that did little to settle the strange churning in her stomach. She needed to call someone, anyone, but the phone felt impossibly distant, as if the connection to her own life had been severed.

[story 2]

Mark stared at the printed report, the fluorescent light of his kitchen casting harsh shadows across the document. The DNA confirmation was undeniable. It wasn't a suggestion; it was a scientific fact laid bare on glossy paper. He kept flexing his jaw, the muscle twitching involuntarily. He walked to the counter, gripping the edge until his knuckles turned white. Every sound in the apartment—the hum of the refrigerator, the distant traffic—seemed amplified, demanding attention. He kept looking back at the paper, his gaze darting from the matching markers to his own reflection in the darkened television screen, searching for some explanation that didn't exist.

[story 3]

When the technician finally explained the findings, Sarah felt a strange detachment, as if she were observing a distant, complicated performance. The data confirmed it: her biological origins led to a family she was entirely unfamiliar with. A wave of profound disorientation washed over her, leaving her feeling strangely lightheaded. She didn't cry; instead, she began methodically straightening the books on the shelf, her movements precise and overly deliberate. The silence of the room pressed in, thick and suffocating, and Sarah found herself needing to pace the length of the living room, a restless energy vibrating just beneath her skin.
